title: Counter-Strike: Team Falcons vs G2 (BO3) - IEM Cologne Major Stage 3
description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Round 1 match between Team Falcons and G2 in the IEM Cologne Major Stage 3, initially scheduled for June 11 at 10:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Team Falcons" if Team Falcons win the match against G2.

This market will resolve to "G2" if G2 win the match against Team Falcons.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Map 2 Total Rounds: Over/Under 15.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike match between Team Falcons and G2 in the IEM Cologne Major Stage 3, initially scheduled for June 11 at 11:05AM ET. This market is decided solely by the total number of rounds played within Map 2 of this match (the combined round score of both teams on that single map), and is independent of which team wins Map 2 or the overall match.

This market will resolve to "Over" if the combined number of rounds played by both teams on Map 2, including any overtime, is greater than 15.5. This market will resolve to "Under" if the combined number of rounds played is less than 15.5. If the combined number of rounds played is exactly 15.5, this market will resolve 50-50.

Map 2 must be played to completion, with a final round score determined by rounds actually played, for this market to resolve to "Over" or "Under". If Map 2 is completed in this way, the market resolves on that round total regardless of how the remainder of the match concludes (including any later forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default). If Map 2 is not played at all, is not played to completion, or is decided by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default rather than by rounds played to a conclusion, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without Map 2 being completed, this market will resolve 50-50.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead, including video evidence.

markets.question: Map Handicap: G2 (-1.5) vs Team Falcons (+1.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Round 1 match between Team Falcons and G2 in the IEM Cologne Major Stage 3, initially scheduled for June 11 at 11:05AM ET.

This market will resolve to "G2" if G2 wins 2 or more maps than Team Falcons in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Team Falcons". 

Maps won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching map being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

eventMetadata.context_description: Falcons enter the IEM Cologne Major 2026 Stage 3 Swiss BO3 as the higher-ranked side with recent momentum from CS Asia Championships appearances and roster stability under new in-game leader karrigan, who has enabled more structured practice for stars NiKo and m0NESY alongside kyousuke and TeSeS. G2 advanced from earlier stages but displayed inconsistency in map execution and aggression, relying on individual talent from huNter- and NertZ. Falcons hold the edge in VRS standing and head-to-head history in this European matchup, where map pool depth, pistol rounds, and late-series execution will determine advancement positioning in the Swiss format.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mexico faces South Africa in the opening match of the 2026 FIFA World Cup Group A on June 11 at Estadio Azteca in Mexico City, giving the co-hosts a significant home-soil edge in their first group-stage fixture. Mexico enters with strong recent form in CONCACAF qualifiers and benefits from familiar conditions at the historic venue that hosted World Cup finals in 1970 and 1986. South Africa, returning to the tournament after a long absence, must navigate a challenging group that also includes South Korea and a playoff winner while adapting to high altitude and the pressure of the ceremonial opener. Historical precedent from their 2010 encounter adds context, though both sides' current preparations, injury reports, and squad depth will shape final lineups closer to kickoff. Trader positioning aligns with Mexico's overall experience and hosting advantages in this early tournament spotlight.

title: Counter-Strike: FURIA vs B8 (BO3) - IEM Cologne Major Stage 3
description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Round 1 match between FURIA and B8 in the IEM Cologne Major Stage 3, initially scheduled for June 11 at 10:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "FURIA" if FURIA win the match against B8.

This market will resolve to "B8" if B8 win the match against FURIA.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Map 1 Total Rounds: Over/Under 24.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike match between FURIA and B8 in the IEM Cologne Major Stage 3, initially scheduled for June 11 at 10:10AM ET. This market is decided solely by the total number of rounds played within Map 1 of this match (the combined round score of both teams on that single map), and is independent of which team wins Map 1 or the overall match.

This market will resolve to "Over" if the combined number of rounds played by both teams on Map 1, including any overtime, is greater than 24.5. This market will resolve to "Under" if the combined number of rounds played is less than 24.5. If the combined number of rounds played is exactly 24.5, this market will resolve 50-50.

Map 1 must be played to completion, with a final round score determined by rounds actually played, for this market to resolve to "Over" or "Under". If Map 1 is completed in this way, the market resolves on that round total regardless of how the remainder of the match concludes (including any later forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default). If Map 1 is not played at all, is not played to completion, or is decided by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default rather than by rounds played to a conclusion, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without Map 1 being completed, this market will resolve 50-50.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead, including video evidence.

markets.description:

This market will resolve to "B8" if B8 win the match against FURIA.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: FURIA enters the IEM Cologne Major 2026 Stage 3 BO3 Swiss opener as the clear favorite, driven by their #5 HLTV ranking and veteran Brazilian core of FalleN, KSCERATO, yuurih, YEKINDAR, and molodoy, whose structure and Major-stage consistency have produced strong recent results. B8, ranked around 15th, carries momentum from three straight 2-1 victories in Stage 2 over BIG, MIBR, and GamerLegion, showcasing improved map execution and individual peaks despite lower overall pedigree. Historical head-to-head leans toward FURIA, though B8 claimed a prior 2026 series win, and the current map veto pool (including Mirage, Inferno, and Nuke) offers B8 realistic upset windows in a best-of-three format. Trader consensus reflects this matchup dynamic, pricing FURIA heavily while acknowledging B8’s form as the primary variable that could extend the series.

title: Elon Musk # tweets June 5 - June 12, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from June 5 12:00 PM ET to June 12, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

title: Stuttgart Open: Martin Landaluce vs Taylor Fritz
description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Martin Landaluce and Taylor Fritz in the Stuttgart Open, originally scheduled for June 10, 2026 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Martin Landaluce' if Martin Landaluce advances against Taylor Fritz.

This market will resolve to 'Taylor Fritz' if Taylor Fritz advances against Martin Landaluce.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Taylor Fritz and Martin Landaluce in the Stuttgart Open, originally scheduled for June 10, 2026 at 4:00AM ET.
This market will resolve to "Fritz" if Taylor Fritz wins by 2 or more sets than Martin Landaluce, based on the final completed score. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Landaluce."
If the match begins but is not completed, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled before play begins or delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a result, this market will also resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official ATP results.

eventMetadata.context_description: Taylor Fritz enters the Stuttgart Open second round as defending champion and the clear favorite against Martin Landaluce, reflecting his superior ranking (No. 9), extensive grass-court experience, and powerful serve-and-rally game that suits the surface. Fritz arrives after a first-round French Open exit but benefits from a bye and strong historical results at this event. Landaluce, the 20-year-old No. 58, secured his first tour-level grass win by rallying past Pierre-Hugues Herbert in the opener, building on recent clay momentum that included a Rome quarterfinal run. This marks their first meeting, with Landaluce still developing on the faster grass courts where Fritz holds a significant edge in experience and firepower.

title: Korea Republic vs. Czechia - More Markets
description: More markets for the FIFA World Cup game, scheduled for June 11 at 10:00 PM ET.

markets.question: Korea Republic vs. Czechia: Both Teams to Score
markets.description:
In the upcoming FIFA World Cup game between Korea Republic and Czechia, scheduled for June 11 at 10:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Korea Republic and Czechia each score at least one goal during the game.

This market will resolve to "No" if either team fails to score (i.e., if one or both teams finish with zero goals).

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on fifa.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead. All markets will settle based on the official final result as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. Revisions to officially declared final scores made after market resolution will not be accounted for in determining the outcome.

eventMetadata.context_description: South Korea and Czechia meet in their FIFA World Cup 2026 Group A opener on June 11 at Estadio Akron in Guadalajara, Mexico, marking Czechia's return to the tournament after a 20-year absence following playoff qualification. South Korea brings its 11th consecutive finals appearance and attacking speed led by Son Heung-min, while Czechia emphasizes defensive organization and set-piece threat. Both sides enter with limited recent competitive form against each other, and the neutral venue plus early tournament positioning make preparation, squad fitness, and tactical adjustments the primary variables shaping outcomes.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mexico and South Africa meet in the 2026 World Cup opener at Estadio Azteca, a matchup marked by balanced squad depth, limited recent high-stakes form for both sides, and no decisive injury or lineup edges reported in official updates. Mexico carries co-host expectations yet enters with a long history of early exits, while South Africa returns to the tournament after years away and faces typical challenges in converting chances against organized defenses. These factors, combined with the high-altitude venue and the pressure of a group-stage kickoff, leave multiple low-to-mid scoring lines like 1-0, 0-0, and 1-1 sharing nearly identical implied probabilities in trader consensus. The result is a tightly contested exact-score market where small shifts in set-piece efficiency or late substitutions could still swing outcomes without favoring any single result.

title: Counter-Strike: Natus Vincere vs Spirit (BO3) - IEM Cologne Major Stage 3
description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Round 1 match between Natus Vincere and Spirit in the IEM Cologne Major Stage 3, initially scheduled for June 11 at 12:30PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Natus Vincere" if Natus Vincere win the match against Spirit.

This market will resolve to "Spirit" if Spirit win the match against Natus Vincere.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Map 3 Rounds Handicap: Spirit (-3.5) vs Natus Vincere (+3.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike match between Spirit and Natus Vincere in the IEM Cologne Major Stage 3, initially scheduled for June 11 at 12:30PM ET. eventMetadata.context_description: Team Spirit enters the IEM Cologne Major 2026 Stage 3 BO3 against Natus Vincere with strong recent momentum, advancing through Stage 2 with convincing wins over 9z, MIBR, and BetBoom while maintaining elite consistency across 2025-2026 events. Spirit holds a clear head-to-head advantage, winning 12 of the last 21 encounters including multiple BO3 victories at prior Majors and IEMs, often exploiting map control and individual star performances from players like donk and sh1ro. Natus Vincere, despite a deep roster featuring b1t, iM, and w0nderful, has shown vulnerability in recent series losses to Spirit, including a 0-2 defeat at IEM Kraków 2026. Both squads feature stable lineups with no reported injuries or changes ahead of the June 11 matchup, though NaVi’s variable map pool execution remains a noted factor in close series.

eventMetadata.context_description: Aurora Gaming holds the edge over Monte in this IEM Cologne Major 2026 Stage 3 Swiss-system BO3 opener due to superior Valve Regional Standings ranking (roughly top 10 versus Monte near #19-22) and stronger overall roster depth featuring experienced players like XANTARES and woxic. Aurora posted a 3-2 record in recent series, including wins over The MongolZ and Parivision, though they dropped maps to MOUZ at PGL Astana. Monte enters on positive momentum after advancing from Stage 2 with a 2-0 win over paiN, yet their lower global standing and limited map pool against higher-tier opposition create a sizable gap. This Swiss opener carries direct playoff implications, with the winner securing early positioning toward the top-eight cutoff.

eventMetadata.context_description: Yastremska enters as the stronger favorite on grass, backed by a higher ranking near No. 49, more consistent recent results, and a 4-0 head-to-head edge including prior wins on varied surfaces. Tomljanovic, ranked around No. 109, snapped a five-match losing streak with a straight-sets first-round victory over Bouzas Maneiro but has shown limited grass-court success lately. The fast, low-bouncing surface favors Yastremska’s aggressive baseline game and serve, while Tomljanovic must rely on experience and flat hitting to stay competitive in what traders view as a winnable but uphill match for the Australian. No major injury concerns have surfaced for either player ahead of the round-of-16 clash.

title: LoL: Bilibili Gaming Junior vs KT Rolster Challengers (BO3) - Asia Masters Group B
description:

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay a Dragon?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. In League of Legends, elemental dragons (Infernal, Mountain, Ocean, Hextech, Chemtech, and Cloud) spawn in the dragon pit starting at 5:00 game time. After being slain, the next dragon spawns 5 minutes later. Once a team claims the Dragon Soul (by slaying four dragons), all subsequent dragon spawns become Elder Dragons.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Bilibili Gaming Junior and KT Rolster Challengers each slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay any elemental dragon during Game 1. Only elemental dragons count toward this market — Elder Dragon kills do not count.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain at least one elemental dragon prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Any Player Quadra Kill?
markets.description:
This market is about whether any player achieves a Quadra Kill during Game 1. In League of Legends, a Quadra Kill occurs when a single player kills 4 enemy champions in rapid succession.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if any player on either team achieves a Quadra Kill during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if no player achieves a Quadra Kill. A Penta Kill (5 kills in rapid succession) also counts as a Quadra Kill having occurred for the purposes of this market.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether a Quadra Kill occurred prior to stoppage. If no Quadra Kill occurred, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 2: Any Player Penta Kill?
markets.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: KT Rolster Challengers enter this Asia Masters 2026 Group B lower-bracket BO3 as clear favorites, reflecting the consistent edge Korean academy squads hold over Chinese LDL teams in international play through superior macro execution and adaptation to the current patch. KT.C sits at a higher global ranking and draws from LCK infrastructure that emphasizes disciplined teamfighting and objective control, while Bilibili Gaming Junior has posted middling results, including a 0-3 loss to WBG Youth in late May and mixed outcomes in recent LDL splits. The Chinese side’s lower world ranking and approximately 47% win rate over the prior three months further underscore the gap. With the match scheduled for June 11, roster stability and patch familiarity will likely determine whether BLG.J can force maps or if KT.C closes out efficiently in straight sets.

markets.question: Libema Open: Nuno Borges vs Marin Cilic
markets.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Nuno Borges and Marin Cilic in the Libema Open, originally scheduled for June 10, 2026 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Nuno Borges' if Nuno Borges advances against Marin Cilic.

This market will resolve to 'Marin Cilic' if Marin Cilic advances against Nuno Borges.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Nuno Borges and Marin Cilic meet in the Libema Open round of 16 on grass in 's-Hertogenbosch, where both advanced with first-round victories. Borges, ranked around No. 50, defeated Terence Atmane in straight sets and enters as the first 2026 ATP grass-season winner at the event, showcasing consistent baseline play and recent improvement on the surface. Cilic, the 37-year-old former world No. 3 and US Open champion ranked No. 47, rallied past Denis Shapovalov in three sets after dropping the opener, highlighting his powerful serve and experience in extended rallies. The closely matched field reflects similar current rankings and limited direct history, with grass-court dynamics and Cilic's veteran serve advantage balanced against Borges' younger legs and recent momentum.

title: Elon Musk # tweets June 9 - June 16, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from June 9 12:00 PM ET to June 16, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders see the 180-239 tweet range as the market frontrunner for Elon Musk’s X activity June 9-16 because his recent daily average has held near 30 posts, fueled by rapid replies, quotes, and commentary on political flashpoints and cultural debates. Verified tracking through June 11 shows consistent volume without slowdowns, aligning with patterns from adjacent weeks where totals exceeded 180 during active news periods. The tight spread between 180-199 and 200-219 reflects uncertainty over whether weekend engagement or breaking events will push the count higher, while lower bands remain discounted given the established pace. Upcoming chart updates and any late-week surges in replies to trending topics remain the key swing factors before resolution.

title: LoL: Misa Esports vs UCAM Esports Club (BO3) - EMEA Masters Group A
description:
This market refers to the LoL Winners match between Misa Esports and UCAM Esports Club in the EMEA Masters Group A, initially scheduled for June 11 at 11:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Misa Esports" if Misa Esports win the match against UCAM Esports Club.

This market will resolve to "UCAM Esports Club" if UCAM Esports Club win the match against Misa Esports.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay Baron Nashor?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay the Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. Baron Nashor is a powerful neutral objective in League of Legends that first spawns at 20:00 game time and respawns 6 minutes after each death, granting the slaying team the Hand of Baron buff.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Misa Esports and UCAM Esports Club each slay Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay Baron Nashor during Game 1.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain Baron Nashor prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay a Dragon?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. In League of Legends, elemental dragons (Infernal, Mountain, Ocean, Hextech, Chemtech, and Cloud) spawn in the dragon pit starting at 5:00 game time. After being slain, the next dragon spawns 5 minutes later. Once a team claims the Dragon Soul (by slaying four dragons), all subsequent dragon spawns become Elder Dragons.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Misa Esports and UCAM Esports Club each slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay any elemental dragon during Game 1. Only elemental dragons count toward this market — Elder Dragon kills do not count.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain at least one elemental dragon prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.description:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if any player on either team achieves a Quadra Kill during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if no player achieves a Quadra Kill. A Penta Kill (5 kills in rapid succession) also counts as a Quadra Kill having occurred for the purposes of this market.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

markets.question: Game 3: Both Teams Slay Baron Nashor?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay the Baron Nashor at least once during Game 3. Baron Nashor is a powerful neutral objective in League of Legends that first spawns at 20:00 game time and respawns 6 minutes after each death, granting the slaying team the Hand of Baron buff.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Misa Esports and UCAM Esports Club each slay Baron Nashor at least once during Game 3. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay Baron Nashor during Game 3.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

markets.question: Game 3: Both Teams Slay a Dragon?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 3. In League of Legends, elemental dragons (Infernal, Mountain, Ocean, Hextech, Chemtech, and Cloud) spawn in the dragon pit starting at 5:00 game time. After being slain, the next dragon spawns 5 minutes later. Once a team claims the Dragon Soul (by slaying four dragons), all subsequent dragon spawns become Elder Dragons.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Misa Esports and UCAM Esports Club each slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 3. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay any elemental dragon during Game 3. Only elemental dragons count toward this market — Elder Dragon kills do not count.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

markets.question: Game 3: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Misa Esports enters the EMEA Masters 2026 Spring Group A BO3 as the clear favorite against UCAM Esports Club, driven by superior regional pedigree from the TCL and stronger recent results. MISA opened the group stage with a clean 2-0 sweep of E WIE EINFACH on June 10, showcasing coordinated macro play and individual carry potential from its roster. UCAM, the second seed from Spain’s LVP SuperLiga, secured a 2-1 win over HMBLE the same day but has shown inconsistency in international play, finishing outside the top placements in prior EMEA Masters appearances. Current trader consensus reflects MISA’s edge in experience, patch adaptation, and head-to-head regional strength, with limited roster turnover or injury concerns altering the matchup outlook.

Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Games Total: O/U 2.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL Winners match between G2 NORD and Galions in the EMEA Masters Group C, initially scheduled for June 11 at 2:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both G2 NORD and Galions each slay Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay Baron Nashor during Game 1.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay a Dragon?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. In League of Legends, elemental dragons (Infernal, Mountain, Ocean, Hextech, Chemtech, and Cloud) spawn in the dragon pit starting at 5:00 game time. After being slain, the next dragon spawns 5 minutes later. Once a team claims the Dragon Soul (by slaying four dragons), all subsequent dragon spawns become Elder Dragons.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both G2 NORD and Galions each slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay any elemental dragon during Game 1. Only elemental dragons count toward this market — Elder Dragon kills do not count.

eventMetadata.context_description: G2 NORD enters this EMEA Masters 2026 Spring Bo3 with strong organizational backing from the G2-NORD merger and a stable roster featuring Shelfmade, Markoon, Toasty, rin, and Tockimo. The German side has posted competitive results in Prime League play and carries momentum from its Winter playoff appearances. Galions counters with an experienced LFL lineup anchored by Carlsen, Thayger, OMON, Harpoon, and Zoelys, emphasizing early skirmish aggression and international roster depth. Both teams arrive with limited direct head-to-head history in the current split, making recent regional form, draft adaptability, and macro execution the primary variables traders are weighing for the implied probabilities in this Group C matchup.

title: Libema Open: Caty McNally vs Solana Sierra
description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Caty McNally and Solana Sierra in the Libema Open, originally scheduled for June 11, 2026 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Caty McNally' if Caty McNally advances against Solana Sierra.

This market will resolve to 'Solana Sierra' if Solana Sierra advances against Caty McNally.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the WTA Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Set 2 Winner: McNally vs Sierra
markets.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Caty McNally and Solana Sierra in the Libema Open, originally scheduled for June 11, 2026 at 10:30AM ET.
This market will resolve to “McNally” if Caty McNally wins set 2. It will resolve to “Sierra” if Solana Sierra wins set 2.
If the match begins but set 2 is not completed with a winner determined (including the match ending before set 2 is reached), this market will resolve 50-50.
If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without play beginning, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Libema Open: Caty McNally vs Solana Sierra
markets.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Caty McNally and Solana Sierra in the Libema Open, originally scheduled for June 11, 2026 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Caty McNally' if Caty McNally advances against Solana Sierra.

This market will resolve to 'Solana Sierra' if Solana Sierra advances against Caty McNally.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the WTA Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Caty McNally enters this Libema Open round-of-16 grass-court clash after a strong comeback victory over fourth seed Emma Navarro, rallying from a set down with dominant second- and third-set performances. The 24-year-old American, ranked around No. 59, brings recent WTA 125 title experience on grass and improved match fitness following earlier 2026 clay-court action at Roland Garros. Solana Sierra, ranked just ahead at No. 56, arrives with momentum from a three-set win over Lois Boisson and solid clay results including French Open wins over Raducanu and Paolini, though her prior head-to-head edge over McNally came on a different surface in Hobart. Grass-court movement, serve effectiveness, and short turnaround factors will likely shape the outcome in this closely matched encounter.

title: Elon Musk # tweets June 12 - June 19, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from June 12 12:00 PM ET to June 19, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders see the June 12–19 window centering near 200–240 tweets as Elon Musk maintains a recent weekly pace of roughly 25–45 posts per day, driven by routine commentary on Tesla deliveries, SpaceX updates, and cultural topics. Recent Polymarket resolutions and trackers show this range reflects baseline activity absent major catalysts. The tight spread across 180–259 outcomes stems from consistent daily volume without the surges tied to launches, earnings, or high-profile controversies that have previously pushed counts above 300. A quiet news cycle or lower engagement on X could anchor results in the middle buckets, while an unscheduled Starship test, regulatory announcement, or viral exchange might lift totals toward 260-plus or suppress them below 200 depending on timing within the seven-day period.

title: LoL: BOMBA Team vs Anubis Gaming (BO3) - EMEA Masters Group D
description:
This market refers to the LoL Elimination match between BOMBA Team and Anubis Gaming in the EMEA Masters Group D, initially scheduled for June 11 at 2:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "BOMBA Team" if BOMBA Team win the match against Anubis Gaming.

This market will resolve to "Anubis Gaming" if Anubis Gaming win the match against BOMBA Team.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Games Total: O/U 2.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL Elimination match between BOMBA Team and Anubis Gaming in the EMEA Masters Group D, initially scheduled for June 11 at 2:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Over" if BOMBA Team and Anubis Gaming play 3 or more games in this series.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay Baron Nashor?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay the Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. Baron Nashor is a powerful neutral objective in League of Legends that first spawns at 20:00 game time and respawns 6 minutes after each death, granting the slaying team the Hand of Baron buff.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both BOMBA Team and Anubis Gaming each slay Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay Baron Nashor during Game 1.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain Baron Nashor prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay a Dragon?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. In League of Legends, elemental dragons (Infernal, Mountain, Ocean, Hextech, Chemtech, and Cloud) spawn in the dragon pit starting at 5:00 game time. After being slain, the next dragon spawns 5 minutes later. Once a team claims the Dragon Soul (by slaying four dragons), all subsequent dragon spawns become Elder Dragons.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both BOMBA Team and Anubis Gaming each slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay any elemental dragon during Game 1. Only elemental dragons count toward this market — Elder Dragon kills do not count.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain at least one elemental dragon prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both BOMBA Team and Anubis Gaming each destroy at least one enemy inhibitor during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) do not destroy any enemy inhibitors.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

markets.question: Game 1: Any Player Quadra Kill?
markets.description:
This market is about whether any player achieves a Quadra Kill during Game 1. In League of Legends, a Quadra Kill occurs when a single player kills 4 enemy champions in rapid succession.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if any player on either team achieves a Quadra Kill during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if no player achieves a Quadra Kill. A Penta Kill (5 kills in rapid succession) also counts as a Quadra Kill having occurred for the purposes of this market.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether a Quadra Kill occurred prior to stoppage. If no Quadra Kill occurred, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Game 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Game 1 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all champion kills recorded during the match by both BOMBA Team and Anubis Gaming. Executions (deaths to non-champion sources such as turrets, minions, or neutral monsters where no enemy champion receives kill credit) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

markets.question: Game 3: Both Teams Slay a Dragon?
markets.description:

After being slain, the next dragon spawns 5 minutes later. Once a team claims the Dragon Soul (by slaying four dragons), all subsequent dragon spawns become Elder Dragons.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both BOMBA Team and Anubis Gaming each slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 3. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay any elemental dragon during Game 3. Only elemental dragons count toward this market — Elder Dragon kills do not count.

eventMetadata.context_description: BOMBA Team enters this EMEA Masters Spring 2026 Group D losers-bracket BO3 off a 0-2 opening loss to Solary, while Anubis Gaming dropped its Round 1 match 0-2 to Eintracht Spandau. The Polish side carries a clear edge in regional pedigree and world ranking (roughly 38-42 versus Anubis near 72), backed by stronger ERL-level competition and recent Rift Legends playoff runs. Anubis, representing the Arabian League with a roster featuring players like Boda, Theocacs, and Giyuu, has shown flashes in MENA play but faces a steep stylistic and experience gap. Trader consensus reflects this hierarchy in implied probabilities for the elimination-stage outcome, with roster stability and form momentum favoring the higher-seeded squad.
